program.name: 530A ("Trump") Accounts
program.description_short: Free government-seeded investment account for children under 18
program.description:
530A ("Trump") Accounts are government-authorized custodial investment accounts for children under 18, created by the 2025 federal tax law (Section 530A of the Internal Revenue Code).

The federal government makes a one-time $1,000 contribution to eligible accounts. To receive the $1,000 government deposit, the child must be a U.S. citizen with a valid Social Security Number who was born between January 1, 2025 and December 31, 2028.

Children born outside this window can still open a Trump Account — they simply will not receive the $1,000 government deposit.

There is no income limit. Accounts are expected to be available starting July 4, 2026. To open an account, a parent or guardian completes IRS Form 4547 on the child's behalf. Only one Trump Account is allowed per child.

Funds in the account must be invested in low-cost index funds or ETFs tracking primarily U.S. equities (e.g., S&P 500), with an expense ratio of no more than 0.10%. Withdrawals are not permitted before the child turns 18. After age 18, the account follows traditional IRA rules.
program.apply_button_description: Learn how to open a Trump Account
program.website_description: Free government-seeded $1,000 investment account for U.S. citizen children born 2025–2028

warning_messages.message: Trump Accounts are not yet open. They are expected to become available on or after July 4, 2026. Check IRS.gov/trumpaccounts for updates.
Your child must have a valid Social Security Number to open a Trump Account. If your child does not yet have an SSN, apply at ssa.gov.

documents.link_text: Apply for a child's SSN at ssa.gov

documents.text: Child's birth certificate or proof of age and citizenship
IRS Form 4547 (to elect to open a Trump Account)
